document_title: PR24 final determinations: Expenditure allowances — Enhancement cost modelling appendix

key_decisions.detail: OLS regression on scheme-level data for most enhancement areas, with random effects for metering and lead pipe replacement. Cook's distance outlier treatment with 4/N threshold
Companies requested £12bn; Ofwat applied scheme-level econometric models to determine efficient costs
Companies requested £2.4bn for growth at sewage treatment works
Companies requested £1.5bn for Industrial Emissions Directive compliance
Companies requested £1.8bn for sanitary parameters improvements
Companies requested over £1bn for supply interconnectors
Companies requested £1.7bn; random effects estimation used
Companies requested £270m; random effects estimation used

key_numbers.label: Outlier threshold
key_numbers.context: Where N is number of observations

glossary_terms.definition: Ordinary Least Squares — regression estimation method used for scheme-level enhancement cost models
Statistical measure of influence of individual observations; 4/N threshold used for outlier identification
Industrial Emissions Directive — EU-derived environmental regulation for certain treatment works
Econometric approach using individual project data rather than aggregated company-level data
Population Equivalent — measure of sewage treatment capacity
